source_name: User curated premium UI execution standards
content:
# User curated premium UI execution standards - Overview

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium UI execution quality gate, frontend generation self-check, technical portfolio visual anchor planning, liquid glass quality validation, non-generic landing page implementation, dashboard and workspace component density, agent handoff record_id reporting
Evidence: direct_spec / high
Source: local-file:D:/Downloads/codex_premium_ui_execution_quality_prompt.txt

Visual traits:
- clear visual concept before coding
- strong hero visual anchor tied to the user domain
- parameter-level CSS tokens for glass, motion, spacing, and states
- project-specific technical visualization instead of generic cards
- component density with meaningful controls, states, and feedback
- light workspace and dark cinematic routing based on task intent
- truthful portfolio content without invented metrics

Use when:
- premium UI execution quality gate
- frontend generation self-check
- technical portfolio visual anchor planning
- liquid glass quality validation
- non-generic landing page implementation
- dashboard and workspace component density
- agent handoff record_id reporting

Avoid when:
- generic dark developer portfolio
- blue-white SaaS template
- plain black background with gray cards
- fake metrics
- emoji icons in premium technical UI
- empty right-side panels
- low-density hero sections
- generic particles as the only visual anchor
- same-size card grids with no hierarchy
- plain skill chips as the main design feature
- calling bg-white/10 backdrop-blur liquid glass without rim, highlight, hierarchy, and states

AI summary: Premium UI execution quality layer for agents. Before coding, choose visual direction, hero pattern, material system, component system, visual anchor, motion strategy, and record_id/rules used. Require strong visual anchors, project-specific technical visualization, parameter-level glass and motion tokens, component density, asset search mapping, truthfulness for portfolio metrics, and a final design self-check to avoid generic templates.

source_name: User curated premium UI execution standards
content:
# User curated premium UI execution standards - Layout Rules

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium UI execution quality gate, frontend generation self-check, technical portfolio visual anchor planning, liquid glass quality validation, non-generic landing page implementation, dashboard and workspace component density, agent handoff record_id reporting
Evidence: direct_spec / high
Source: local-file:D:/Downloads/codex_premium_ui_execution_quality_prompt.txt

- Before coding, state the selected visual direction, hero pattern, material system, component system, visual anchor, motion strategy, and database record_id used.
- Every premium hero needs a strong visual anchor such as a topology diagram, waveform, task queue, retrieval graph, dashboard cluster, device map, or animated SVG/canvas panel.
- For technical portfolios, map project domains to project-specific visuals: STM32 uses waveform/sensor stream, RK3588 uses topology/process pipeline, ML compensation uses residual curves and before-after charts, automation tools use queues/logs/checkpoints, AI database work uses retrieval graphs and domain routing panels.
- Landing pages need hero, navbar, CTA, tagline pill, information cluster, section cards, and motion reveal.
- Workspace and app pages need command/search, glass tabs or filters, status chips, KPI or project cards, toast/modal, loading shimmer, and hover/focus/active states.
- Avoid repeating safe templates; do not default every premium portfolio to dark developer cards unless the user requested dark/cinematic styling.
- Route landing and SaaS homepage tasks to video hero, cinematic hero, and premium typography rules; route dashboards and workspaces to the Liquid Glass Web App UI Kit; route technical portfolios to project-specific visual anchors and this execution quality layer.

AI summary: Premium UI execution quality layer for agents. Before coding, choose visual direction, hero pattern, material system, component system, visual anchor, motion strategy, and record_id/rules used. Require strong visual anchors, project-specific technical visualization, parameter-level glass and motion tokens, component density, asset search mapping, truthfulness for portfolio metrics, and a final design self-check to avoid generic templates.

source_name: User curated premium UI execution standards
content:
# User curated premium UI execution standards - Interaction States

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium UI execution quality gate, frontend generation self-check, technical portfolio visual anchor planning, liquid glass quality validation, non-generic landing page implementation, dashboard and workspace component density, agent handoff record_id reporting
Evidence: direct_spec / high
Source: local-file:D:/Downloads/codex_premium_ui_execution_quality_prompt.txt

- background reveal opacity 0 to 1 with blur 18px to 0 over 600ms
- hero reveal translateY 34px, blur 12px, 800ms, around 100ms delay
- toolbar reveal translateY 22px, blur 8px, 620ms, around 190ms delay
- card reveal translateY 28px, scale 0.975, blur 10px, 660ms, stagger 80ms to 140ms
- toast spring-in translateX 24px to 0 over 440ms
- loading shimmer 1.2s to 1.6s ease-in-out infinite
- hover, focus, active, loading, disabled, error, and success states must be present when components are interactive
- respect prefers-reduced-motion and do not leave persistent will-change

AI summary: Premium UI execution quality layer for agents. Before coding, choose visual direction, hero pattern, material system, component system, visual anchor, motion strategy, and record_id/rules used. Require strong visual anchors, project-specific technical visualization, parameter-level glass and motion tokens, component density, asset search mapping, truthfulness for portfolio metrics, and a final design self-check to avoid generic templates.

source_name: User curated premium UI execution standards
content:
# User curated premium UI execution standards - Accessibility Notes

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium UI execution quality gate, frontend generation self-check, technical portfolio visual anchor planning, liquid glass quality validation, non-generic landing page implementation, dashboard and workspace component density, agent handoff record_id reporting
Evidence: direct_spec / high
Source: local-file:D:/Downloads/codex_premium_ui_execution_quality_prompt.txt

- Do not rely on glow alone for selected, error, success, or disabled states.
- Respect prefers-reduced-motion for reveal and shimmer patterns.
- Keep glass text contrast readable and provide stronger fallback fills where transparency harms readability.
- Use placeholder labels such as TBD or omit unknown portfolio metrics instead of inventing credibility.
- Report whether metrics are placeholders in the final handoff.

AI summary: Premium UI execution quality layer for agents. Before coding, choose visual direction, hero pattern, material system, component system, visual anchor, motion strategy, and record_id/rules used. Require strong visual anchors, project-specific technical visualization, parameter-level glass and motion tokens, component density, asset search mapping, truthfulness for portfolio metrics, and a final design self-check to avoid generic templates.

source_name: User curated premium UI execution standards
content:
# User curated premium UI execution standards - Implementation Hints

Page type: component_pattern
Use cases: premium UI execution quality gate, frontend generation self-check, technical portfolio visual anchor planning, liquid glass quality validation, non-generic landing page implementation, dashboard and workspace component density, agent handoff record_id reporting
Evidence: direct_spec / high
Source: local-file:D:/Downloads/codex_premium_ui_execution_quality_prompt.txt

- Use light glass tokens: blur(18px) saturate(160%), blur(24px) saturate(180%), blur(28px) saturate(185%), fill rgba(255,255,255,0.48) to rgba(255,255,255,0.85), bright rim, inset highlight, outer shadow, and radii 22px/28px/40px/999px.
- Use dark neon glass tokens: rgba(8,10,16,0.78), rgba(15,23,42,0.64), white rim 0.18 to 0.42, cyan/magenta/orange glints, deep shadow, inset gloss, and neon ring.
- Use motion tokens: cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1), cubic-bezier(0.2,1.2,0.2,1), and cubic-bezier(0.2,1.25,0.25,1).
- Search ui_assets for visual anchors when motion, video, dashboard preview, technical diagram, Lottie, hover motion, or background inspiration is needed.
- For inspiration_only or review_required assets, recreate the effect with original CSS, SVG, Canvas, Lottie configuration, or self-authored animation rather than copying files.
- Final output should report record_id/rules used, asset usage policy, direct-use versus inspiration-only status, placeholder metrics, and whether the result avoids generic template layout.

AI summary: Premium UI execution quality layer for agents. Before coding, choose visual direction, hero pattern, material system, component system, visual anchor, motion strategy, and record_id/rules used. Require strong visual anchors, project-specific technical visualization, parameter-level glass and motion tokens, component density, asset search mapping, truthfulness for portfolio metrics, and a final design self-check to avoid generic templates.
